mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding household income and consumption, lifting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are negative in > 50% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% signals the zero lower bound constraint, forcing investors out of cash and Treasuries into risk assets and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production indicate broad-based demand momentum that exceeds supply capacity, pushing corporate pricing power and margins higher and generating positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and INDPRO rise month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return <= 0 over a 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: During high unemployment regimes, a month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals labor market healing and reduced tail risk for consumer spending, generating outsized positive equity returns as risk premiums compress over the next week.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, month-over-month declines in UNRATE are followed by negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 55% of observations over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects manufacturing sector momentum that translates into higher corporate earnings surprises, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in > 50% of instances over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1,000 thousand units signal a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for durables and construction materials, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in > 50% of instances when HOUST is below 1,000 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint month-over-month increase in CPI inflation alongside a federal funds rate below 0.25% indicates deeply negative real rates, which stimulate short-term risk-taking and lift equity prices over the next week.
failure_condition: In months where CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in > 55% of cases over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP captures the economy's fundamental trajectory; positive growth surprises reflect stronger-than-expected aggregate demand, lifting corporate profit expectations and S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in > 50% of quarters over any rolling 5-year window.
